// owner_chat_id: Numeric Telegram chat ID of the bot's owner. Files saved to owner-style paths only for this chat. Get yours by DMing @userinfobot.
// allowed_chat_ids: If non-empty, bot ignores messages from any chat NOT in this list. Use to lock the bot to specific people/groups. Leave [] to allow everyone.
// default_quality: Default video resolution cap for non-live downloads (TikTok, YouTube videos, IG, etc.). Per-chat overrides via /default_video_size. Accepted: 'best' / '1080p' / '720p' / '360p' / 'audio-only'.
// max_concurrent_downloads: Semaphore for parallel jobs. Higher = faster bulk but more bandwidth + disk contention.
// delete_after_send: If true, deletes the local file after successful Telegram send. Saves disk for bots used purely as a relay; leave false if you want a local archive.
// temp_ttl_hours: Cleanup interval for /downloads/temp/ (used for allowed-chat downloads). Files older than this get reaped.
// live_enabled: Master switch. If false, live URLs return 'live recording disabled'.
// live_max_concurrent: Per-host cap on simultaneous live recordings. Higher = more parallel streams but more CPU/bandwidth.
// live_heartbeat_seconds: Hint for how often yt-dlp's progress hook emits. A separate 60s timer-based reporter ALSO updates the UI from disk, so progress is visible regardless.
// live_min_free_disk_gb: Refuse to start a live recording if free disk is below this. Prevents fill-the-disk surprises on multi-hour streams.
// live_abort_on_session_fail: Zero-retry on auth/cookie failures. Recommended true — retrying a failed cookie wastes hours.
// live_platforms: Advisory only. Any URL with a yt-dlp extractor is allowed; this list is just for labelling.
// live_max_height: Resolution cap for live recordings. Trades file size for quality. 1080p60 ≈ 56 MB/min, 720p60 ≈ 25 MB/min, 480p ≈ 12 MB/min. 0 = unlimited (source quality).
// live_transcode_height: Optional post-recording re-encode. 0 = off. 480 or 240 = re-encode after finalize. CPU-expensive on long recordings.
// live_transcode_keep_original: If true, the original is kept alongside the transcoded sibling (archive + small-for-delivery). If false, transcode replaces original.
// monitor_enabled: Master switch for the polling loop that watches /watch-list URLs and DMs you when they go live.
// monitor_poll_interval_seconds: How often the watchlist is probed. Lower = faster live detection, higher API cost on the watched sites.
// monitor_probe_timeout_seconds: Max wait for a yt-dlp probe before treating the entry as offline.
